verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease with no canonical TM domain or GPI anchor. It is historically characterized as secreted, but a 2025 landmark study (Shen et al., Clin Cancer Res) confirmed robust cell-surface localization in prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP by FACS) and patient-derived mCRPC tumors by multiplex immunofluorescence and confocal antibody-internalization imaging. This has enabled clinical-stage surface-directed therapeutics: pasritamig (KLK2×CD3 bispecific T-cell engager, Phase 3), 225Ac-KLK2 radioligand therapy (Phase 1), and CAR-T. Surface display is restricted to prostate cancer cells in the androgen-receptor-signaling context; the mechanism of anchoring is unresolved (no TM domain identified). Normal HPA annotation is cytoplasmic/secretory. Thus verdict is contextual/cell_state_induced (oncogenic prostate cancer-state outer-leaflet display) not 'yes', as the surface pool is cancer-state-restricted, not baseline. No evidence for lysosomal exocytosis, stable TM-partner tethering, germline-only display, or dual-localization PM cycling in non-cancer cells.
